spec_match.rationale: The page now reflects the current Parse Rotation algorithm: arbitrary numeric input is rounded to the nearest quarter turn, ties round toward positive infinity, and the result is normalized modulo 360.

interactive.rationale: The built-in candidate table runs on load and the custom input lets users probe arbitrary numeric values.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Copy, title, table labels, and results all describe rotation rounding instead of incorrectly claiming invalid numeric rotations throw.

references.rationale: Includes ChromeStatus and WebCodecs spec links.

design_system.rationale: Uses the local demo styling without raw CSS color regressions.

summary: Verified in Chrome 148 with chrome-devtools-mcp: built-in cases stored the expected rounded rotations and custom value 135 stored frame.rotation=180 with no console errors.
